items.hook_line: A real boutique homestay does not hook you with decor first. It hooks you the moment the gate opens and your pace changes.
items.target_scene: Standalone courtyard properties, old-town houses, or small design homestays with a clear arrival gate and visible shift from outside to inside.
items.cover_direction: Lead the cover with the gate, sign, and first courtyard view, with a room glimpse or luggage motion as support. Make the promise about entering a different pace.
first_3_shots: Local anchor to property gate
Gate push or courtyard entry with property identity
Key handoff or luggage into the hero room
items.fallback_if_missing: If the arrival chain is incomplete, pivot to a room-truth route and drop the switch-of-state promise.

items.hook_line: The value is not only that the neighborhood is lively. The value is that you can come back from it and feel quiet immediately.
items.target_scene: Old-town, lane, riverside, or arts-district boutique homestays where the room really reads as calm after the local environment.
items.cover_direction: Use one local anchor and one quiet room frame on the cover. The title should answer how the property balances energy outside and calm inside.
first_3_shots: Neighborhood anchor tied to the property entrance
Return path from outside into the property
Quiet room proof with bed, window, or tea corner
items.fallback_if_missing: If route proof is weak, keep only the room-comfort angle and stop promising lively-then-quiet contrast.

items.hook_line: The stay feels different when you can see that someone actually prepared to receive you.
items.target_scene: Host-led boutique homestays with real welcome gestures, local recommendation moments, or handmade breakfast coverage.
items.cover_direction: Put the host gesture, welcome card, or breakfast handoff on the cover with a small room inset. Sell a real receiving moment, not generic service.
first_3_shots: Host or welcome note establishes the relationship
Breakfast or tea prep shows real service
Room or dining table closes the proof loop
items.fallback_if_missing: If host-led proof is missing, move back to room and space credibility instead of claiming warm host energy.

items.hook_line: The best boutique room is not one that only photographs well. It is one that still feels usable when you stay in it.
items.target_scene: Design-forward rooms with strong materials and styling that also have clean bathrooms, usable surfaces, and clear room logic.
items.cover_direction: Use a wide room frame as the main visual with a bathroom or tabletop detail inset. Promise a room that looks good and holds up as a stay.
first_3_shots: Room wide shot establishes layout
Table, outlet, lamp, or luggage detail proves usability
Bathroom or bedding confirms comfort and upkeep
items.fallback_if_missing: If liveability proof is thin, downgrade to a space preview instead of a design-stay claim.

items.hook_line: The real question is not whether the view is nice. The question is whether the room makes you want to sit down and stay longer.
items.target_scene: Rooms with a window view, terrace, tea corner, reading corner, or calm pause zone that genuinely belongs to the hero room.
items.cover_direction: Show the bed and window or tea corner in one believable relationship. Make the title about staying, not just looking.
first_3_shots: Hero room wide shot with bed and pause zone relation
Tea, book, bag-drop, or pause gesture detail
Bathroom, night lamp, or morning light completes the stay rhythm
items.fallback_if_missing: If the pause zone and room relation are not clear, reduce the promise to a simple room-view preview.

items.hook_line: Night atmosphere only works when the path back to the room feels as real as the lights.
items.target_scene: Boutique homestays with real night access to courtyards, rooftops, terraces, or lit public areas and a clear return-to-room path.
items.cover_direction: Put the night courtyard or terrace on the cover, but anchor it with a room door, corridor, or return-path cue.
first_3_shots: Night courtyard or terrace establishes open-state reality
Path lighting and room-return logic make the mood executable
Night room state closes the proof chain
items.fallback_if_missing: If there is no real night window, shift to a daytime or early-evening route and drop the night-mood hook.

items.hook_line: A boutique homestay becomes memorable when the life outside the door feels connected to the stay inside.
items.target_scene: Properties that truly connect to a lane, village road, riverside path, market, or neighborhood rhythm right outside the door.
items.cover_direction: Use the door and one local anchor together. The promise should be about living in the place, not just visiting around it.
first_3_shots: Property entrance tied to a local anchor
Short movement into or out of the neighborhood
Return to the room or courtyard so the place claim lands on the stay
items.fallback_if_missing: If the connection between place and property is weak, stop at local atmosphere and do not frame it as a doorstep-life stay.

items.hook_line: Some boutique homestays do not sell distance. They sell a one-night reset that actually feels complete.
items.target_scene: Urban escape or near-destination stays with enough assets to cover arrival, room pause, and breakfast or departure.
items.cover_direction: Build the cover from arrival, the hero room, and a morning cue. Make the promise about a complete one-night rhythm.
first_3_shots: Arrival identity and entry transition
Room pause or comfort zone
Breakfast, morning light, or departure closure
items.fallback_if_missing: If the closure assets are missing, reduce the story to a room preview and arrival guide instead of a full reset narrative.
